name: Gingerbread Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the applesauce, brown sugar, and molasses together thoroughly.
2. Stir in 1/2 cup cold water and vanilla.
3. Combine the dry ingredients (flours, spices, baking soda, and salt) in a separate bowl.
4. Add the dry ingredients to the wet mixture and stir in an additional tablespoon of cold water until well combined.
5. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate the dough for at least one hour.
6. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
7. Roll out the chilled dough on a lightly floured surface to about 1/2 inch thickness.
8. Cut out cookie shapes using a glass or cutter with a 1-2 inch diameter.
9. Place the cookies on an ungreased baking sheet, spacing them about 2 inches apart.
10. Bake for approximately 10 minutes, or until lightly browned and set when touched lightly with a finger.
Note: This recipe is from Sonja and William Conner's The New American Diet (pp. 365).
